title: Our Lady of Salambao

infobox.value: Juan, Julián, and Diego dela Cruz
San Pascual Baylón Parish Church, Obando, Bulacan,

([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language_in_the_Philippines "Spanish language in the Philippines"): *Nuestra Señora de la Inmaculada Concepción de Salambáo*; [Tagalog](/wiki/Tagalog_language "Tagalog language"): *Mahal na Birhen ng Salambáo* or *Birheng ng Salambáw*) is a [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_the_Philippines "Catholic Church in the Philippines") [title](/wiki/Titles_of_Mary "Titles of Mary") of the [Blessed Virgin Mary](/wiki/Blessed_Virgin_Mary "Blessed Virgin Mary") venerated in [Obando, Bulacan](/wiki/Obando,_Bulacan "Obando, Bulacan"), [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"). The Virgin Mary under this title is venerated as the local [patroness](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of fishing, owing to the image's discovery in a *[salambáw](/wiki/Salamb%C3%A1w "Salambáw")*, a type of large [lift net](/wiki/Lift_net "Lift net") supported by bamboo crosspieces and mounted on a raft.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Our Lady of The Immaculate Conception of Salambao | |
| --- | --- |
| Closeup of the image of Our Lady of Salambáo, showing her ensconced in a *salambáw* net. | |
| Location | [Obando](/wiki/Obando,_Bulacan "Obando, Bulacan"), [Bulacan](/wiki/Bulacan "Bulacan"),    [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines") |
| Date | 19 June 1763 |
| Witness | Juan, Julián, and Diego dela Cruz |
| Type | Finding of the image |
| Shrine | San Pascual Baylón Parish Church, Obando, Bulacan, |

**Our Lady of Salambáo** ([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language_in_the_Philippines "Spanish language in the Philippines"): *Nuestra Señora de la Inmaculada Concepción de Salambáo*; [Tagalog](/wiki/Tagalog_language "Tagalog language"): *Mahal na Birhen ng Salambáo* or *Birheng ng Salambáw*) is a [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_the_Philippines "Catholic Church in the Philippines") [title](/wiki/Titles_of_Mary "Titles of Mary") of the [Blessed Virgin Mary](/wiki/Blessed_Virgin_Mary "Blessed Virgin Mary") venerated in [Obando, Bulacan](/wiki/Obando,_Bulacan "Obando, Bulacan"), [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"). The Virgin Mary under this title is venerated as the local [patroness](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of fishing, owing to the image's discovery in a *[salambáw](/wiki/Salamb%C3%A1w "Salambáw")*, a type of large [lift net](/wiki/Lift_net "Lift net") supported by bamboo crosspieces and mounted on a raft.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

The image is enshrined with [Saint Clare of Assisi](/wiki/Saint_Clare_of_Assisi "Saint Clare of Assisi") and [Saint Paschal Baylon](/wiki/Saint_Paschal_Baylon "Saint Paschal Baylon") inside [Obando Church](/wiki/Obando_Church "Obando Church"). The three saints form a triad that is the focus of the annual [Obando Fertility Rites](/wiki/Obando_Fertility_Rites "Obando Fertility Rites") held from 17 to 19 May.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)

## Legend

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Our_Lady_of_Salambao&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Legend")]

The date of the image's finding is traditionally held to be 19 June 1763. Three fishermen, named Juan, Julián, and Diego de la Cruz, were fishing at a place known as *Hulingduong* in the town of Tambobong (now [Malabon](/wiki/Malabon "Malabon") City). There, they caught the statue within their *salambáw*, which is a large type of fishing [lift net](/wiki/Lift_net "Lift net") made from bamboo crosspieces and mounted on a raft. When the fishermen tried to bring the Virgin's image to neighbouring [Navotas](/wiki/Navotas "Navotas"), their *salambáw* suddenly grew heavy and their boat immobile. They decided to head for Obando, and their raft lightened and became easy to row. This they took as a sign the Virgin wanted to be enshrined in Obando.

The image—complete with a replica *salambáw*—is now housed in a wooden *retablo* ([reredos](/wiki/Reredos "Reredos")) above the high altar of San Pascual Baylón Parish, together with statues of [Saint Clare](/wiki/Saint_Clare_of_Assisi "Saint Clare of Assisi") and [Saint Paschal](/wiki/Saint_Paschal_Baylon "Saint Paschal Baylon"). A replica of the statue, in its own *salambáw*, is used for the annual procession on the final day of the Rites.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

## Feast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Our_Lady_of_Salambao&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Feast")]

The feast of Our Lady of Salambáo is on 19 May, which is the last day of a [triduum](/wiki/Triduum "Triduum") honouring the triad of saints. Spread across the triduum are the Rites, which constitute [Masses](/wiki/Mass_(liturgy) "Mass (liturgy)") and [processions](/wiki/Procession "Procession") where devotees of both sexes joyfully dance the *[fandango](/wiki/Fandango "Fandango")* in supplication for a child and of good livelihood and harvest.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) The celebrations are centered on the San Pascual Baylón Parish Church, also known as the National Shrine of Nuestra Señora de la Inmaculada Concepción de Salambáo in Barangay Pag-asa, Obando, Bulacan.
